title: AI Engineer III – AI Enablement
company_focus: Healthcare insurance and managed care services
description:
The AI Engineer III – AI Enablement Technical Lead is a
senior individual contributor and the primary hands-on
technical leader within AITE. This role is accountable for
the technical direction, solution design quality, and
implementation integrity of AI-enabled solutions and AITE-
owned automation capabilities.

Consistent with CareSource’s AI Engineer III career level,
this role operates independently on complex, ambiguous
problems; sets technical direction within its domain; and
leads design and delivery without day-to-day oversight.

This role does not own or operate core AI platforms or
runtime infrastructure. You will partner closely with AI
Platform, Infrastructure, and SRE teams by providing
solution requirements, architectural input, and design
feedback while maintaining a clear separation between
solution design (AITE) and platform delivery and operations.

Technical leadership and solution architecture

- Lead solution-level architecture for AI initiatives,
  including data, models, orchestration, security,
  integration, and observability
- Define and maintain reference architectures, design
  patterns, and technical standards for AI-enabled solutions
- Translate business problems into actionable designs and
  clearly communicate tradeoffs, risks, and constraints
- Provide technical decision support and recommendations to AI
  and IT leadership

Hands-on engineering and validation

- Build proofs of concept and pilots, and implement critical
  solution components to de-risk delivery
- Develop and validate RAG pipelines, agent-based
  architectures, and orchestration workflows
- Contribute production-quality code and configurations when
  hands-on intervention is needed to unblock delivery
- Evaluate emerging AI capabilities, tools, and services
  through targeted experimentation

Enablement, guidance, and oversight

- Provide technical guidance to delivery teams, architects,
  and engineers implementing AI solutions
- Conduct architecture and design reviews to ensure alignment
  with enterprise architecture, security, and governance
  standards
- Mentor engineers and architects on AI design patterns,
  implementation practices, and operational considerations
- Review vendor-proposed designs and implementations for
  alignment with CareSource standards

AITE automation and enablement capabilities

- Design, develop, and implement internal automations that
  support AITE intake, governance workflows, reporting, and
  operational visibility
- Translate documented process specifications into durable,
  reusable automation capabilities
- Own the technical design and evolution of AITE’s internal
  enablement tooling as the team expands
- Architect automation solutions to scale with increased AI
  demand, additional governance requirements, and broader
  AITE scope

Governance and Responsible AI by design

- Embed governance and Responsible AI requirements into
  solution architectures from inception
- Partner with Security, Risk, Legal, and AI Governance
  stakeholders to ensure compliant implementations
- Define solution-level controls for data protection,
  auditability, and appropriate human oversight

Platform partnership (non-operational)

- Provide solution-driven requirements and architectural
  feedback to AI Platform and Infrastructure teams
- Collaborate on standards for LLMOps, observability,
  reliability, and cost management without owning platform
  operations

Education and experience

- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a
  related technical field required; equivalent experience
  accepted in lieu of degree
- 7+ years of hands-on software or systems engineering
  experience
- 3+ years designing and implementing AI-enabled or advanced
  analytics solutions in an enterprise environment
- Demonstrated experience acting as a senior technical lead
  across multiple teams and stakeholders

Technical skills and expertise

- AI solution architecture: strong grasp of modern AI patterns
  including RAG, agentic workflows, orchestration layers,
  and evaluation approaches
- Hands-on engineering: strong Python skills and modern
  software engineering practices (testing, version control,
  CI/CD as applicable)
- Azure AI ecosystem: experience designing solutions using
  Azure AI Services (Azure OpenAI, AI Search, Azure Machine
  Learning, Azure AI Foundry)
- Integration and APIs: ability to design secure, scalable
  system integrations
- Security-aware design: working knowledge of identity,
  network isolation, private endpoints, and enterprise
  security constraints
- Evaluation and observability: ability to define success
  metrics and interpret performance, cost, latency, and
  reliability signals
- Automation engineering (AITE-owned): experience designing
  workflow automation and operational tooling (for example,
  Power Platform, SharePoint, and service workflow
  integrations)

Leadership and communication

- Ability to explain technical concepts and tradeoffs to
  technical and non-technical audiences
- Strong written documentation skills for architectures,
  standards, and technical guidance
- Comfort influencing across organizational boundaries without
  direct authority

Certifications (Preferred)

- Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate or
  equivalent
- Azure Solutions Architect Expert (preferred)

Working Conditions

- General office environment; hybrid-eligible
- Occasional extended periods of sitting or standing
- Travel not typically required

Compensation Range:

$94,100.00 - $164,800.00

CareSource takes into consideration a combination of a
candidate’s education, training, and experience as well as
the position’s scope and complexity, the discretion and
latitude required for the role, and other external and
internal data when establishing a salary level. In addition
to base compensation, you may qualify for a bonus tied to
company and individual performance. We are highly invested
in every employee’s total well-being and offer a substantial
and comprehensive total rewards package.

Compensation Type (hourly/salary):

Salary

Organization Level Competencies

- Fostering a Collaborative Workplace Culture
- Cultivate Partnerships
- Develop Self and Others
- Drive Execution
- Influence Others
- Pursue Personal Excellence
- Understand the Business



This job description is not all inclusive. CareSource
reserves the right to amend this job description at any
time. CareSource is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are
dedicated to fostering an environment of belonging that
welcomes and supports individuals of all backgrounds.#LI-GM1
responsibilities: Lead solution-level architecture for AI initiatives including data, models, orchestration, security, integration, and observability
Build proofs of concept and pilots, and implement critical solution components including RAG pipelines and agent-based architectures
Define and maintain reference architectures, design patterns, and technical standards for AI-enabled solutions
Provide technical guidance and mentorship to delivery teams, architects, and engineers implementing AI solutions
Design, develop, and implement internal automations supporting AITE intake, governance workflows, reporting, and operational visibility
Embed governance and Responsible AI requirements into solution architectures from inception
Partner with AI Platform and Infrastructure teams to provide solution-driven requirements and architectural feedback
Evaluate emerging AI capabilities, tools, and services through targeted experimentation
use_cases: RAG pipeline development and validation for enterprise knowledge retrieval
Agent-based AI architectures for workflow automation and orchestration
AI-enabled internal governance and intake process automation
Enterprise AI solution design for healthcare managed care operations
Responsible AI compliance and auditability in healthcare solutions
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves hands-on building and validating RAG
pipelines, agent-based architectures, and orchestration
workflows. While titled "AI Enablement," the engineer
directly implements AI-enabled solutions, builds proofs of
concept, and develops production-quality AI components,
making this an ai-first role.
